title: Withdraw ₳592,780 for Beyond Minimum Viable Governance: Iteratively Improvin....

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (enacted), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "This treasury withdrawal funds Beyond Minimum Viable Governance: Iteratively Improving on Cardano Voltaire which will provide the following services: This initiative will establish a robust framework for evaluating and enhancing Cardano's governance system." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting; Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections. Residual uncertainty: Dossier completeness not confirmed for this treasury proposal. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 20.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional voting is blocked until dossier quality gates pass.

Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.
Provide: Missing dossier section: complete proposal summary

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw ₳592,780 for Beyond Minimum Viable Governance: Iteratively Improvin....

Deliverables: This treasury withdrawal funds Beyond Minimum Viable Governance: Iteratively Improving on Cardano Voltaire which will provide the following services: This initiative will establish a robust framework for evaluating and enhancing Cardano's governance system., We will identify and benchmark key governance metrics to assess the health, performance, and overall impact of our decentralized decision-making processes., The resulting 'State of Governance' report will provide a structured evaluation of participation, track vital Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), map the evolving governance ecosystem, and pinpoint specific areas for improvement., To ensure our findings are grounded in real-world experience, we will engage the Cardano community through various channels.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This treasury withdrawal funds Beyond Minimum Viable Governance: Iteratively Improving on Cardano Voltaire which will provide the following services: This initiative will establish a robust framework for evaluating and enhancing Cardano's governance system.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): We will identify and benchmark key governance metrics to assess the health, performance, and overall impact of our decentralized decision-making processes.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The resulting 'State of Governance' report will provide a structured evaluation of participation, track vital Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), map the evolving governance ecosystem, and pinpoint specific areas for improvement.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): To ensure our findings are grounded in real-world experience, we will engage the Cardano community through various channels.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): This proposal aims to solve the following problem: Cardano stands at the forefront of decentralized governance innovation.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): Yet, we face a critical challenge: the absence of transparent, data-backed frameworks to evaluate our governance's effectiveness, pinpoint obstacles, and drive impactful improvements.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "This treasury withdrawal funds Beyond Minimum Viable Governance: Iteratively Improving on Cardano Voltaire which will provide the following services: This initiative will establish a robust framework for evaluating and enhancing Cardano's governance system." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open.
Strongest NO: an unresolved blocker (missing complete proposal summary) means costs or weak controls may outweigh the claimed benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
Gate disbursement on milestones that must be met before money moves.
Provide a clawback or refund path if the work is not delivered.
missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity
cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence
delivery controls, milestones, assurance, or remedy path

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
